span.p: rate creep - actual rates above budget

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Projected overage identified on cost report

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the overage in above-the-line or below-the-line accounts?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Route to EP - ATL overages require deal renegotiation or EP approval - not a BTL cut decision

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the BTL overage in a locked cost - agreed rate or signed contract?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does contingency cover the locked overage?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Access contingency - document the cause - update the cost report - no schedule change needed

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Escalate to EP and financier - options are schedule compression, reduced scope, or additional funding - produce a trade-off memo

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the overage from schedule slippage or from rate creep?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Schedule review - tighten the one-liner - look for location grouping and company-move savings before cutting scenes

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Rate audit - compare deal memos to budget - flag any rates approved above budget and re-forecast
